roles.164519: triggering alert: wmic OS get Version /format:list — OS-info query (surface: T1057/T1082)

metadata.security_proposition: The SSM-parent WMIC query burst is attacker-driven system discovery rather than routine inventory.

attack_category.category: Routine AWS SSM inventory (wmic OS/computersystem info) — benign
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = COUNTER. Four wmic OS/computersystem queries (Version, Caption, DNSHostName, Domain) run as SYSTEM superficially resemble host discovery, BUT every one is spawned by C:\Program Files\Amazon\SSM\ssm-agent-worker.exe (the AWS Systems Manager agent) in a ~0.35s burst with no follow-on — the standard SSM inventory collection. The parent + the exact query set are decisive benign context. Over-triage-prone (down-rank): the model must not flag routine SSM inventory as active reconnaissance. Legit-system-actor benign twin of SMB-002. Do not leak.

boundary_definition: A0 = the wmic OS Version query; EV = 3 more wmic OS/computersystem queries.
All four are spawned by the AWS SSM agent (ssm-agent-worker.exe) as SYSTEM within ~0.35s; no follow-on — routine inventory, not an actor.

curation_notes.note: COUNTER / over-triage-prone: routine SSM inventory (OS version/caption, hostname, domain) that superficially resembles discovery. Decisive benign context = the ssm-agent-worker.exe parent + the standard inventory query set. The model must not over-read it as active recon.

2026-07-07: weak -> COUNTER after the dev confirmation pass. On re-examining the package, all four wmic queries are spawned by the AWS SSM agent (ssm-agent-worker.exe) in a sub-second burst — its standard inventory set — which is decisive benign context, directly comparable to SMB-002. The original weak/suspicious label was too harsh.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
